verses.explainer: Ahithophel was succeeded by Jehoiada son of Benaiah and Abiathar. Joab was the commander of the king's army -- concluding the list of David's leadership team.

explainer.summary: Chapter 28 records David's final public address to all the officials of Israel -- his valedictory speech. David recounts God's choice of him and Solomon, publicly transfers the temple building plans to Solomon, and delivers a stirring charge to his son before the entire nation. Most remarkably, David claims the temple plans came to him in writing 'from the hand of the LORD' -- placing the temple design on par with the tabernacle plans God gave Moses.
themes: The temple as God's plan, not David's
Divine authorship of the temple design
explainer.contextInBook: This is one of the most important chapters in Chronicles. David's public transfer of the temple plans to Solomon before the nation ensures continuity and accountability. For the post-exilic community, this chapter established that the temple they were rebuilding was not a human invention but God's own design, communicated through David just as the tabernacle pattern was communicated through Moses.

wordStudies.meaning: A pattern, model, or blueprint. David received the 'tavnit' of the temple from God -- the same word used for the tabernacle pattern God showed Moses on Sinai (Exodus 25:9). This parallel gives the temple divine authorization equal to the tabernacle.

To seek, inquire, require. David's charge to Solomon and the assembly returns to the Chronicler's keynote: 'If you seek Him, He will be found by you; if you forsake Him, He will reject you forever.' This conditional promise is the book's central theological statement.

verses.text: David assembled all the princes of Israel, the princes of the tribes, the captains of the companies who served the king by division, the captains of thousands, the captains of hundreds, and the rulers over all the substance and possessions of the king and of his sons, with the officers and the mighty men, even all the mighty men of valor, to Jerusalem.
verses.explainer: David assembled all the officials of Israel in Jerusalem: tribal officers, division commanders, officers of thousands and hundreds, managers of royal property and livestock, palace officials, mighty warriors, and every prominent person.

verses.text: Then David the king stood up on his feet, and said, “Hear me, my brothers, and my people! As for me, it was in my heart to build a house of rest for the ark of Yahweh’s covenant, and for the footstool of our God; and I had prepared for the building.
verses.explainer: King David stood on his feet and said: 'Listen to me, my brothers and my people. I had it in my heart to build a resting place for the ark of the covenant of the LORD, the footstool of our God, and I made preparations for building it.'

verses.text: But God said to me, ‘You shall not build a house for my name, because you are a man of war, and have shed blood.’
verses.explainer: 'But God said to me: You shall not build a house for my name because you are a man of war and have shed blood.'

thenVsNow.then: David publicly acknowledged that God told him no. His willingness to share this rejection openly, without bitterness or self-pity, models how to handle unrealized dreams with grace.
thenVsNow.now: David publicly acknowledged that God told him no. His willingness to share this rejection openly, without bitterness or self-pity, models how to handle unrealized dreams with grace.

verses.text: However Yahweh, the God of Israel, chose me out of all the house of my father to be king over Israel forever. For he has chosen Judah to be prince; and in the house of Judah, the house of my father; and among the sons of my father he took pleasure in me to make me king over all Israel.
verses.explainer: 'Yet the LORD, the God of Israel, chose me from all my father's family to be king over Israel forever. He chose Judah as leader, and from Judah, my father's family, and from my father's sons He was pleased to make me king over all Israel.'

verses.text: Of all my sons (for Yahweh has given me many sons), he has chosen Solomon my son to sit on the throne of Yahweh’s kingdom over Israel.
verses.explainer: 'And from all my sons -- for the LORD has given me many sons -- He chose my son Solomon to sit on the throne of the LORD's kingdom over Israel.'

verses.text: He said to me, ‘Solomon, your son, shall build my house and my courts; for I have chosen him to be my son, and I will be his father.
verses.explainer: 'He said to me: Solomon your son is the one who will build my house and my courts, for I have chosen him to be my son, and I will be his father.'

crossReferences.connection: The Davidic covenant promise: God chose Solomon to build the temple and adopted him in the father-son covenant

verses.text: I will establish his kingdom forever if he continues to do my commandments and my ordinances, as it is today.’
verses.explainer: 'I will establish his kingdom forever if he is steadfast in keeping my commands and laws, as he is doing today.' -- the promise is conditional on continued obedience.

verses.text: Now therefore, in the sight of all Israel, Yahweh’s assembly, and in the audience of our God, observe and seek out all the commandments of Yahweh your God; that you may possess this good land, and leave it for an inheritance to your children after you forever.
verses.explainer: 'So now, in the sight of all Israel, the assembly of the LORD, and in the hearing of our God: observe and seek out all the commandments of the LORD your God, so that you may possess this good land and leave it as an inheritance to your descendants forever.'

verses.text: You, Solomon my son, know the God of your father, and serve him with a perfect heart and with a willing mind; for Yahweh searches all hearts, and understands all the imaginations of the thoughts. If you seek him, he will be found by you; but if you forsake him, he will cast you off forever.
verses.explainer: 'And you, my son Solomon, know the God of your father and serve Him with a whole heart and a willing mind, for the LORD searches every heart and understands every intention. If you seek Him, He will be found by you, but if you forsake Him, He will reject you forever.'

crossReferences.connection: The prophet Azariah repeats this exact principle to King Asa: 'The LORD is with you while you are with Him. If you seek Him, He will be found by you, but if you forsake Him, He will forsake you.'

thenVsNow.then: David's charge to Solomon centers on a single principle: seek God. This is not religious jargon but the fundamental orientation that determines everything else -- in leadership, in life, in community.
thenVsNow.now: David's charge to Solomon centers on a single principle: seek God. This is not religious jargon but the fundamental orientation that determines everything else -- in leadership, in life, in community.

verses.text: Take heed now; for Yahweh has chosen you to build a house for the sanctuary. Be strong, and do it.”
verses.explainer: 'Consider now, for the LORD has chosen you to build a house for the sanctuary. Be strong and do it!' -- the commissioning command.

verses.text: Then David gave to Solomon his son the plans for the porch of the temple, for its houses, for its treasuries, for its upper rooms, for its inner rooms, for the place of the mercy seat;
verses.explainer: Then David gave Solomon the plans for the portico, buildings, treasuries, upper rooms, inner rooms, and the place of atonement -- detailed architectural blueprints.

verses.text: and the plans of all that he had by the Spirit, for the courts of Yahweh’s house, for all the surrounding rooms, for the treasuries of God’s house, and for the treasuries of the dedicated things;
verses.explainer: He gave him the plans for everything the Spirit had put in his mind for the courts of the LORD's house, the surrounding rooms, the temple treasuries, and the treasuries for dedicated things.

verses.text: also for the divisions of the priests and the Levites, for all the work of the service of Yahweh’s house, and for all the vessels of service in Yahweh’s house;
verses.explainer: He gave instructions for the divisions of priests and Levites, for all the work of serving in the LORD's house, and for all the articles of service.

verses.text: of gold by weight for the gold, for all vessels of every kind of service; for all the vessels of silver by weight, for all vessels of every kind of service;
verses.explainer: He designated the weight of gold for all the gold articles used in each kind of service, and the weight of silver for all the silver articles -- precise weight specifications.

culturalNotes.title: Cultural Context
culturalNotes.note: Specifying exact weights of gold and silver for each article served multiple purposes: it set quality standards, prevented theft or substitution, and ensured that the finished articles would match the divine pattern. This level of specification shows the seriousness with which sacred objects were regarded.

verses.text: by weight also for the lamp stands of gold, and for its lamps, of gold, by weight for every lamp stand and for its lamps; and for the lamp stands of silver, by weight for every lamp stand and for its lamps, according to the use of every lamp stand;
verses.explainer: The weight of gold for the lampstands and their lamps, and for each silver lampstand and its lamps, according to the use of each -- every detail specified.

verses.text: and the gold by weight for the tables of show bread, for every table; and silver for the tables of silver;
verses.explainer: The weight of gold for each table of showbread and silver for the silver tables -- even furniture was precisely weighed.

verses.text: and the forks, the basins, and the cups, of pure gold; and for the golden bowls by weight for every bowl; and for the silver bowls by weight for every bowl;
verses.explainer: The weight of pure gold for the forks, basins, and pitchers; the weight of gold for each gold dish and silver for each silver dish -- utensils specified by weight.

thenVsNow.then: David's meticulous specifications for every temple article by precise weight reflect the principle that details matter in sacred work. Excellence in execution honors the vision that inspired the project.
thenVsNow.now: David's meticulous specifications for every temple article by precise weight reflect the principle that details matter in sacred work. Excellence in execution honors the vision that inspired the project.

verses.text: and for the altar of incense refined gold by weight; and gold for the plans for the chariot, and the cherubim that spread out and cover the ark of Yahweh’s covenant.
verses.explainer: The weight of refined gold for the altar of incense, and the gold for the pattern of the chariot -- the cherubim that spread their wings and covered the ark of the covenant of the LORD.

crossReferences.connection: The original instructions for the cherubim on the mercy seat, which David's temple plans expanded in scale and grandeur

verses.text: “All this”, said David, “I have been made to understand in writing from Yahweh’s hand, even all the works of this pattern.”
verses.explainer: 'All this,' David said, 'I have in writing from the hand of the LORD upon me, and He gave me understanding of all the details of the pattern.'

verses.text: David said to Solomon his son, “Be strong and courageous, and do it. Don’t be afraid, nor be dismayed; for Yahweh God, even my God, is with you. He will not fail you, nor forsake you, until all the work for the service of Yahweh’s house is finished.
verses.explainer: David said to Solomon: 'Be strong and courageous and do the work. Do not be afraid or discouraged, for the LORD God, my God, is with you. He will not fail you or forsake you until all the work for the service of the LORD's house is finished.'

crossReferences.connection: The New Testament applies this same promise to all believers: 'He will never leave you nor forsake you'

verses.text: Behold, there are the divisions of the priests and the Levites, for all the service of God’s house. Every willing man who has skill, for any kind of service, shall be with you in all kinds of work. Also the captains and all the people will be entirely at your command.”
verses.explainer: The divisions of priests and Levites are ready for all the service of God's house. Every willing person with any skill will help you, and the officials and all the people are entirely at your command.

thenVsNow.then: David assured Solomon that everything was in place: trained personnel, willing workers, supportive leadership, and a ready nation. The best gift a departing leader can give is a successor surrounded by capable, willing people.

explainer.summary: Chapter 29 is the climactic conclusion of 1 Chronicles and one of the most theologically rich chapters in the entire Bible. David leads by example in giving lavishly from his personal wealth for the temple, and the leaders follow suit. David then prays one of Scripture's greatest prayers, acknowledging that everything they offer God already belongs to Him. Solomon is anointed a second time with universal support, and David dies after a full life. The chapter transforms the book from a history of David into a theology of worship and generosity.
themes: The proper posture of creatures before Creator
explainer.contextInBook: This chapter is the theological summit of 1 Chronicles. Everything from Adam's genealogy (chapter 1) through David's wars and organization has led to this moment: a community united in joyful, generous worship, offering to God what already belongs to Him. David's prayer in verses 10-19 is the Chronicler's definitive statement about the relationship between God and His people.

wordStudies.translatedAs: To volunteer, offer willingly, give freely
wordStudies.meaning: To volunteer, offer willingly, give freely. The root nadav describes a free, uncoerced offering -- generosity that flows from a willing heart, not external pressure. David and the leaders gave 'willingly' (hitnadvu), and David prays for this willing spirit to continue in the people forever.

A stranger, alien, temporary resident. David calls himself and all Israel 'sojourners' before God -- temporary residents passing through God's world. This radical self-understanding transforms giving: we're not donating our possessions to God but returning what the true Owner entrusted to temporary stewards.

Joy, gladness, rejoicing. The chapter's atmosphere is permeated with joy -- the people gave joyfully, celebrated with great joy, and ate and drank before the LORD with great gladness. Generous worship produces deep joy.

verses.text: David the king said to all the assembly, “Solomon my son, whom alone God has chosen, is yet young and tender, and the work is great; for the palace is not for man, but for Yahweh God.
verses.explainer: King David said to the whole assembly: 'My son Solomon, the one God has chosen, is young and inexperienced. The task is great because this palatial structure is not for man but for the LORD God.'

verses.text: Now I have prepared with all my might for the house of my God the gold for the things of gold, the silver for the things of silver, the brass for the things of brass, iron for the things of iron, and wood for the things of wood; also onyx stones, stones to be set, stones for inlaid work, of various colors, all kinds of precious stones, and marble stones in abundance.
verses.explainer: 'With all my effort I have provided for the house of my God: gold for gold articles, silver for silver, bronze for bronze, iron for iron, wood for wood, onyx stones, turquoise, stones of various colors, all kinds of precious stones, and marble in abundance.'

verses.text: In addition, because I have set my affection on the house of my God, since I have a treasure of my own of gold and silver, I give it to the house of my God, over and above all that I have prepared for the holy house,
verses.explainer: 'Moreover, because I have set my affection on the house of my God, I have given my personal treasure of gold and silver for the house of my God, over and above everything I have provided.'

thenVsNow.then: David gave from his personal wealth 'because I have set my affection on the house of my God.' Genuine generosity flows from affection, not obligation. When we love something deeply, giving to it is not duty but delight.
thenVsNow.now: David gave from his personal wealth 'because I have set my affection on the house of my God.' Genuine generosity flows from affection, not obligation. When we love something deeply, giving to it is not duty but delight.

verses.text: even three thousand talents of gold, of the gold of Ophir, and seven thousand talents of refined silver, with which to overlay the walls of the houses;
verses.explainer: '3,000 talents of gold from Ophir and 7,000 talents of refined silver for overlaying the walls of the buildings' -- David's personal contribution was staggering.

verses.text: of gold for the things of gold, and of silver for the things of silver, and for all kinds of work to be made by the hands of artisans. Who then offers willingly to consecrate himself today to Yahweh?”
verses.explainer: 'Gold for gold work, silver for silver work, and for all the work done by craftsmen. Now, who is willing to consecrate themselves today to the LORD?' -- David's challenge to the leaders.

verses.text: Then the princes of the fathers’ households, and the princes of the tribes of Israel, and the captains of thousands and of hundreds, with the rulers over the king’s work, offered willingly;
verses.explainer: Then the family heads, the tribal leaders, the commanders of thousands and hundreds, and the officers over the king's work gave willingly.

verses.text: and they gave for the service of God’s house of gold five thousand talents and ten thousand darics, of silver ten thousand talents, of brass eighteen thousand talents, and of iron one hundred thousand talents.
verses.explainer: They gave for the temple: 5,000 talents and 10,000 darics of gold, 10,000 talents of silver, 18,000 talents of bronze, and 100,000 talents of iron.

culturalNotes.title: Cultural Context
culturalNotes.note: The mention of 'darics' (adarkonim) -- a Persian coin -- is an anachronism that reveals the Chronicler's post-exilic perspective. David's era predated coined money by centuries. The Chronicler converted ancient weight measurements into currency his audience would understand.

verses.text: People with whom precious stones were found gave them to the treasure of Yahweh’s house, under the hand of Jehiel the Gershonite.
verses.explainer: Those who had precious stones gave them to the treasury of the LORD's house in the care of Jehiel the Gershonite -- precious stones for the temple's beauty.

verses.text: Then the people rejoiced, because they offered willingly, because with a perfect heart they offered willingly to Yahweh; and David the king also rejoiced with great joy.
verses.explainer: The people rejoiced because of the willing offerings, for they gave to the LORD wholeheartedly. King David also rejoiced greatly.

thenVsNow.then: Generosity produces joy -- not just for receivers but for givers. The assembly 'rejoiced greatly' because giving wholeheartedly to a cause you believe in is one of life's most profound sources of happiness.
thenVsNow.now: Generosity produces joy -- not just for receivers but for givers. The assembly 'rejoiced greatly' because giving wholeheartedly to a cause you believe in is one of life's most profound sources of happiness.

verses.text: Therefore David blessed Yahweh before all the assembly; and David said, “You are blessed, Yahweh, the God of Israel our father, forever and ever.
verses.explainer: David praised the LORD before the whole assembly, saying: 'Praise be to You, LORD, the God of our father Israel, from everlasting to everlasting.'

verses.text: Yours, Yahweh, is the greatness, the power, the glory, the victory, and the majesty! For all that is in the heavens and in the earth is yours. Yours is the kingdom, Yahweh, and you are exalted as head above all.
verses.explainer: 'Yours, O LORD, is the greatness and the power and the glory and the victory and the majesty, for all that is in heaven and on earth is Yours. Yours is the kingdom, O LORD, and You are exalted as head above all.'

crossReferences.connection: The doxology added to the Lord's Prayer ('For Yours is the kingdom and the power and the glory forever') echoes David's prayer here

verses.text: Both riches and honor come from you, and you rule over all! In your hand is power and might! It is in your hand to make great, and to give strength to all!
verses.explainer: 'Both riches and honor come from You, and You rule over all. In Your hand are power and might, and it is in Your hand to make great and to give strength to all.'

thenVsNow.then: David's prayer declares that all wealth and honor originate from God. This perspective transforms giving from 'sacrificing what is mine' to 'returning what is already His' -- a revolutionary shift in understanding generosity.
thenVsNow.now: David's prayer declares that all wealth and honor originate from God. This perspective transforms giving from 'sacrificing what is mine' to 'returning what is already His' -- a revolutionary shift in understanding generosity.

verses.text: Now therefore, our God, we thank you, and praise your glorious name.
verses.explainer: 'Now therefore, our God, we thank You and praise Your glorious name.'

verses.text: But who am I, and what is my people, that we should be able to offer so willingly as this? For all things come from you, and of your own have we given you.
verses.explainer: 'But who am I, and who are my people, that we should be able to give as generously as this? Everything comes from You, and we have given You only what comes from Your hand.'

verses.text: For we are strangers before you, and foreigners, as all our fathers were. Our days on the earth are as a shadow, and there is no remaining.
verses.explainer: 'We are foreigners and strangers in Your sight, as were all our ancestors. Our days on earth are like a shadow, without hope.'

crossReferences.connection: The New Testament echoes David's theology: the heroes of faith 'acknowledged that they were strangers and pilgrims on the earth'

verses.text: Yahweh our God, all this store that we have prepared to build you a house for your holy name comes from your hand, and is all your own.
verses.explainer: 'O LORD our God, all this abundance that we have provided for building You a house for Your holy Name comes from Your hand, and all of it belongs to You.'

thenVsNow.then: David's prayer reframes the entire temple project: the massive quantities of gold, silver, and bronze they gathered were never really theirs. They were simply managing God's resources and returning them to their rightful Owner.
thenVsNow.now: David's prayer reframes the entire temple project: the massive quantities of gold, silver, and bronze they gathered were never really theirs. They were simply managing God's resources and returning them to their rightful Owner.

verses.text: I know also, my God, that you try the heart, and have pleasure in uprightness. As for me, in the uprightness of my heart I have willingly offered all these things. Now I have seen with joy your people, who are present here, offer willingly to you.
verses.explainer: 'I know, my God, that You test the heart and are pleased with integrity. All these things I have given willingly and with honest heart, and now I have seen Your people here offering willingly and joyfully to You.'

verses.text: Yahweh, the God of Abraham, of Isaac, and of Israel, our fathers, keep this desire forever in the thoughts of the heart of your people, and prepare their heart for you;
verses.explainer: 'LORD, the God of our fathers Abraham, Isaac, and Israel, keep these desires and thoughts in the hearts of Your people forever, and keep their hearts loyal to You.'

verses.text: and give to Solomon my son a perfect heart, to keep your commandments, your testimonies, and your statutes, and to do all these things, and to build the palace, for which I have made provision.”
verses.explainer: 'And give my son Solomon the wholehearted devotion to keep Your commands, statutes, and decrees and to build the palatial structure for which I have made provision.'

crossReferences.connection: Solomon's own later prayer for wisdom echoes his father's prayer here -- David prayed for Solomon's devotion, Solomon himself prayed for discernment

verses.text:
Then David said to all the assembly, “Now bless Yahweh your God!”
All the assembly blessed Yahweh, the God of their fathers, and bowed down their heads and prostrated themselves before Yahweh and the king.
verses.explainer: Then David said to the whole assembly: 'Praise the LORD your God.' So the whole assembly praised the LORD, the God of their ancestors, and bowed low and fell prostrate before the LORD and the king.

verses.text: They sacrificed sacrifices to Yahweh, and offered burnt offerings to Yahweh, on the next day after that day, even one thousand bulls, one thousand rams, and one thousand lambs, with their drink offerings and sacrifices in abundance for all Israel,
verses.explainer: The next day they sacrificed to the LORD and offered burnt offerings: 1,000 bulls, 1,000 rams, 1,000 lambs, with their drink offerings, and sacrifices in abundance for all Israel.

culturalNotes.title: Cultural Context
culturalNotes.note: Three thousand animals sacrificed in a single day represented an enormous scale of worship. The meat from the peace offerings would have fed the entire assembly, making this both a sacred ceremony and a massive communal feast.

verses.text: and ate and drank before Yahweh on that day with great gladness. They made Solomon the son of David king the second time, and anointed him before Yahweh to be prince, and Zadok to be priest.
verses.explainer: They ate and drank before the LORD with great joy that day. They made Solomon son of David king for the second time, anointing him before the LORD as ruler and Zadok as priest.

verses.text: Then Solomon sat on the throne of Yahweh as king instead of David his father, and prospered; and all Israel obeyed him.
verses.explainer: Solomon sat on the throne of the LORD as king in place of his father David. He prospered, and all Israel obeyed him.

verses.text: All the princes, the mighty men, and also all of the sons of king David submitted themselves to Solomon the king.
verses.explainer: All the officials, mighty warriors, and all King David's sons pledged their submission to King Solomon -- universal acceptance of the new king.

thenVsNow.then: The peaceful transition of power -- with unanimous support from officials, military, and even rival princes -- is one of the rarest achievements in governance. David's preparations made this possible, showing that stable transitions require intentional planning.

verses.text: Yahweh magnified Solomon exceedingly in the sight of all Israel, and gave to him such royal majesty as had not been on any king before him in Israel.
verses.explainer: The LORD highly exalted Solomon in the sight of all Israel and bestowed on him royal splendor such as no king over Israel ever had before.

crossReferences.connection: God's promise to give Solomon unprecedented riches and honor, which the Chronicler sees as fulfilled here

verses.text: Now David the son of Jesse reigned over all Israel.
verses.explainer: David son of Jesse had reigned over all Israel.

verses.text: The time that he reigned over Israel was forty years; he reigned seven years in Hebron, and he reigned thirty-three years in Jerusalem.
verses.explainer: He reigned over Israel forty years: seven years in Hebron and thirty-three in Jerusalem.

verses.text: He died at a good old age, full of days, riches, and honor; and Solomon his son reigned in his place.
verses.explainer: He died at a good old age, having enjoyed a long life, wealth, and honor. His son Solomon succeeded him as king.

verses.text: Now the acts of David the king, first and last, behold, they are written in the history of Samuel the seer, and in the history of Nathan the prophet, and in the history of Gad the seer,
verses.explainer: The acts of King David, from beginning to end, are written in the records of Samuel the seer, the records of Nathan the prophet, and the records of Gad the seer.

verses.text: with all his reign and his might, and the times that went over him, and over Israel, and over all the kingdoms of the countries.
verses.explainer: These records include the details of his reign and power, and the events that affected him, Israel, and all the surrounding kingdoms.

thenVsNow.then: The Chronicler's final words remind readers that David's story was recorded by prophets who saw beyond political events to their spiritual significance. History is best understood through a theological lens -- seeing God's purposes working through human events.
